title: picea obovata var. japonica
intro: Picea obovata var. japonica, also known as Japanese Siberian spruce, is a tree species native to Japan and Russia. It is a coniferous evergreen tree with needle-like leaves and a conical shape, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The medicinal properties of Picea obovata var. japonica include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, making it useful for treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Picea obovata var. japonica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to promote respiratory health and reduce inflammation, thereby offering benefits for cardiovascular health and immune system function.
